choiceresponse.label: You want to explain to a clinician how thal_3 and cp_0 affect the prediction. Which statements are true? (Select all that apply.)

checkboxgroup.choice: From this importance table alone, you can tell whether thal_3 increases or decreases predicted risk.
The importance table tells you how useful each feature is to the forest overall, but not how it affects any particular patient.
A feature can be highly important even if it is not strongly correlated with the outcome linearly.
Feature importance values can be interpreted as the probability the feature is “correct.”
To get directionality, you would need a different tool (e.g., partial dependence, SHAP, or examining individual tree paths).

div.p: Random Forest impurity-based importances are global and directionless. They indicate that a feature helps split the data into purer subsets, but do not say whether larger values increase or decrease disease likelihood. Because forests capture nonlinearities and interactions, a feature may be important even without a strong linear correlation. For directionality or patient-specific explanations, you need tools like SHAP, partial dependence plots, or local path-based explanations.
